claim: YOLO demand router prd_readiness already creates blockers when evidence agents report missing, clarify, block, or failed status; missing and clarify recommendations cannot produce prd_ready true.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function creates EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING blocker when missing data is reported (lines 496-502), and creates EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED or EVIDENCE_AGENT_BLOCKED blocker when recommendation is clarify/blocked/block or verdict is blocked/failed (lines 503-509).
evidence.why: This is the core logic that ensures evidence agent recommendations become blockers.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues for each complete evidence agent result and adds all returned issues to the blocker list (lines 726-732).
evidence.why: This confirms that blocking issues from evidence agents are added to the PRD readiness blocker list.

evidence.summary: Required evidence agent roles (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) are enforced by adding EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers when any required role has not completed with evidence and a result (lines 717-725).
evidence.why: This confirms that all required evidence agents must complete before PRD readiness can be true.

evidence.summary: prd_ready is set to blockers.length === 0, meaning PRD readiness is true only when no blockers exist (line 756).
evidence.why: This confirms that any blocker, including those from evidence agents, prevents prd_ready from being true.

evidence.summary: Test 'evidence agent missing data and clarify recommendations block PRD readiness' verifies that when cross-checker reports missing data and recommends clarify, PRD readiness is false and blockers include EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING and EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED (lines 350-352).
evidence.why: This test proves the current behavior matches the desired outcome.

evidence.summary: Test 'cross-check PRD readiness requires every evidence agent result' verifies that when only explorer completes but cross-checker and verifier are missing, PRD readiness is false (lines 170-172).
evidence.why: This test confirms that missing required evidence agents block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: actionableMissingItems function filters out non-actionable status notes like 'no missing data identified' or '100% agreement' to avoid treating them as actual missing items (lines 98-110).
evidence.why: This explains how the router distinguishes between actual missing data and status summaries.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-missing status notes in missing arrays do not block readiness' verifies that status notes like 'No missing data identified' and 'All required evidence tasks completed' do not block PRD readiness (lines 632).
evidence.why: This test confirms the router correctly filters non-actionable status notes.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles function returns ['explorer', 'cross-checker', 'verifier'] for cross_check policy, and ['explorer', 'verifier'] for single_agent policy (lines 475-479).
evidence.why: This defines which evidence agents are required based on evidence policy.

evidence.summary: If any evidence agent recommends cross_check, the required roles are expanded to include explorer, cross-checker, and verifier (lines 710-716).
evidence.why: This shows that cross_check requests dynamically upgrade the required evidence agents.

risks: If this claim is wrong, the demand could generate a PRD from incomplete evidence.

result.notes: The current implementation already satisfies the demand. Evidence agent missing/clarify/block/failed statuses create blockers that prevent prd_ready from being true. The tests confirm this behavior works as intended.
